global_cues.us_markets: US markets closed lower on April 21. S&P 500 -0.63% to 7,064.01; Nasdaq -0.59% to 24,259.96 (snapping a 13-day win streak earlier); Dow -0.59% (-293 pts) to 49,149.38. Weakness driven by uncertainty over whether a US-Iran peace deal will be reached before the ceasefire expires today (April 22).
global_cues.european_markets: European markets were cautious amid US-Iran ceasefire uncertainty and elevated crude oil prices; risk-off tone carried from Wall Street.
global_cues.asian_markets: GIFT Nifty was +0.41% at 24,440.5 late on April 21, suggesting a mildly positive open. Broader Asia mixed: Hang Seng had been weak (-1.0% last session), CSI300 -0.17%. Nikkei trading without strong directional cue given oil-driven risk aversion.

fii_dii_summary: April 21: FIIs net SELLERS of Rs 1,918.99 Cr (cash); DIIs net BUYERS of Rs 2,221.27 Cr (cash). DII absorption offset FII selling, helping Nifty close +0.87% at 24,576.60. Trend of FII outflows persists amid oil spike and rupee weakness, while DIIs remain supportive.
macro_events: US-Iran ceasefire expires TODAY (April 22) - outcome of peace talks is the single biggest swing factor for risk assets and crude
RBI MPC (April 8) held repo rate at 5.25%, neutral stance; FY27 GDP projected 6.9% (cut from 7.4% Feb), CPI 4.6%
RBI flagged West Asia conflict and elevated crude as primary headwinds to growth outlook
Q4 FY26 earnings season in full swing - heavy IT and financials docket this week
sector_themes: IT: Tech Mahindra Q4 today and Infosys tomorrow will set tone for IT pack after TCS numbers; margin commentary and FY27 guidance key
Banking/PSU: Govt opening SBI MD role to private sector candidates; bank unions opposing - watch PSU bank sentiment
Oil & Gas/OMCs: WTI ~$87-88/bbl; crude spike pressures marketing margins at HPCL/BPCL/IOC, supports upstream (ONGC, Oil India)
Pharma: Novo Nordisk weighing Wegovy rollout in India - catalyst for obesity/diabetes theme (Sun Pharma, Cipla, Biocon)
Insurance/NBFC: SBI Life earnings today; Tata Capital on April 23
Consumer/Retail: Trent Q4 today - read-through for discretionary spend
Capital Goods/EMS: Havells Q4 today - read for consumer durables and power sector
risk_flags: US-Iran ceasefire expiration today April 22 - binary event; breakdown could spike Brent toward $95-100 and further weaken INR
USD/INR near 93.00 - multi-decade weakness; pressures importers, IT neutral-to-positive, adds imported inflation risk
WTI crude at ~$87-88/bbl (recently +5% on Monday after -11.5% Friday) - extreme volatility; every $10 rise in crude adds ~30bps to CPI
FII outflows continuing - Rs 1,919 Cr sold yesterday; sustained selling risks pressure on high-beta midcaps and financials
US markets snapped multi-day rally; global risk-off could hit Indian tech/exporters if extended
Geopolitical: West Asia conflict flagged by RBI as upside risk to inflation and downside risk to growth
brief_summary: Markets face a binary catalyst today with the US-Iran ceasefire expiration, set against soft US close and elevated crude near $88. GIFT Nifty signals a mildly positive open (+0.4%), but IT (Tech Mahindra) and financials (SBI Life) earnings plus FII selling (Rs 1,919 Cr) warrant cautious positioning. Bias: trade light, avoid fresh longs in oil-sensitive names (OMCs, paints, aviation) until ceasefire outcome is clear; DII support and IT earnings are the offsets.
